page: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations place the m7a.8xlarge in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. It demonstrates strong capabilities in specialized tasks, including software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory performance is robust, with write bandwidth for large blocks and read bandwidth for cached small blocks ranking in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read bandwidth falls into the middle 50%. The server also excels in low-latency operations, achieving top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference and strong results in Redis SET operations and database tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, utilizing high-performance DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPUs. This makes it highly efficient for workloads that rely on external block storage and require consistent, non-burstable compute power. Recommended workloads include database operations, software development and compilation pipelines, web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top 25% for single-core and multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and database operations, alongside top-tier results in small-model LLM inference. The m7a.8xlarge is highly suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, database management, software build pipelines, and medium-scale web serving where dedicated, high-frequency CPU performance and modern DDR5 memory bandwidth are required without local storage overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 12 Gbps network baseline, optimized for database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong CPU and database performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server delivering balanced DDR5 memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU and database benchmarks
Top-tier prompt processing and text generation for small LLM models
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
